rationale: Recent CEX/DEX negative-news batch covering Mango Markets, Nobitex, WazirX, Phemex, and Upbit. Mango Markets is modeled as dead / regulation after its SEC settlement and January 2025 wind-down. Nobitex is modeled as limited after the June 2025 politically motivated hack and temporary offline state, not dead because a terminal shutdown is not confirmed. WazirX is modeled as active with major 2024-2025 hack, suspension, restructuring, and restart events. Phemex and Upbit are modeled as active with hack / temporary suspension / restoration events because both reported or showed service restoration rather than permanent closure.

entities.summary: Solana-based decentralized exchange and lending/trading protocol that wound down after a 2022 exploit, 2024 SEC/CFTC-related regulatory settlements, and January 2025 governance changes that made borrowing and new positions economically unviable.
entities.notes: Classified as dead / regulation because the terminal wind-down followed SEC settlement and regulatory/legal pressure, with governance actions effectively ending core borrowing/lending/trading activity. The 2022 exploit is important background but not the immediate death_reason.

entities.summary: Iranian centralized cryptocurrency exchange. In June 2025 it was hit by a politically motivated hack attributed to Predatory Sparrow / Gonjeshke Darande, with roughly USD 90 million in crypto burned or destroyed and the website/app taken offline during incident review.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead yet. Reuters and other sources report a major hack and offline website/app state, but current evidence does not prove permanent shutdown, liquidation, or service death. Keep as limited unless a later closure or durable recovery marker is confirmed.

entities.summary: India-focused centralized cryptocurrency exchange that suspended operations after a July 2024 USD 230-235 million hack, then restarted trading in October 2025 after a Singapore High Court-sanctioned restructuring scheme. It remains active but carries major hack, suspension, restructuring, and restart events.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead. The platform had a long suspension after the 2024 hack, but official and news sources confirm court-sanctioned restructuring and phased restart in October 2025.

entities.summary: Centralized exchange that suffered a hot-wallet security incident in January 2025, temporarily suspended deposits and withdrawals, and later restored withdrawal services. It is modeled as active with major incident events rather than a death-side record.
entities.notes: Official Phemex timeline is the primary source for the incident, suspension, staged restoration, and final February 2025 withdrawal restoration.

entities.summary: South Korean centralized exchange operated by Dunamu. In November 2025 it suffered a Solana hot-wallet / abnormal withdrawal incident involving roughly USD 30-40 million in assets, suspended deposits and withdrawals, and later resumed Solana-related services in phases.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead. The 2025 Solana incident was severe, but reporting and exchange notices describe reimbursement, suspended transfers, and staged resumption rather than terminal closure.

events.title: SEC settled charges against Mango Markets entities
events.description: The SEC filed settled charges against Mango DAO, Blockworks Foundation, and Mango Labs over unregistered offers/sales of MNGO tokens and unregistered broker activity connected to the Mango Markets platform.
events.notes: Primary regulatory precursor to the 2025 wind-down.

events.title: Mango Markets announced it would shut down
events.description: Mango Markets announced that users should close positions and that Mango v4 / Boost were winding down, with governance proposals set to make most borrowing economically unviable.
events.notes: Announcement date follows reporting of Mango's public January 11 statement.

events.title: Mango Markets wind-down proposals became executable
events.description: Governance proposals tied to the wind-down became executable on January 13, 2025, effectively ending core borrowing/lending/trading viability on Mango v4 / Boost.
events.notes: HEI uses the proposal execution date as the terminal operational marker.

events.title: Nobitex suffered a politically motivated hack
events.description: Nobitex was attacked by hackers identifying as Predatory Sparrow / Gonjeshke Darande, with roughly USD 90 million in crypto assets transferred to inaccessible or burned addresses and source-code exposure threatened.
events.notes: The event is politically motivated and destructive rather than normal theft for profit, according to multiple sources.

events.title: Nobitex website and app were taken offline
events.description: Nobitex said it pulled its website and app offline while reviewing unauthorized access to its systems and hot wallet infrastructure after the attack.
events.notes: Modeled as withdrawal_suspended because site/app offline and system review imply service interruption, but not enough for dead classification.

events.title: WazirX suffered a USD 230-235 million hack
events.description: WazirX suffered a large hack in July 2024 that drained roughly USD 230-235 million from the platform and led to a long suspension and restructuring process.
events.notes: Loss amount varies slightly by source; keep approximate language.

events.title: WazirX halted withdrawals and trading after the breach
events.description: Following the July 2024 breach, WazirX suspended platform operations including withdrawals and trading, producing a roughly 15-16 month interruption before phased restart.
events.notes: This is the main service-interruption marker.

events.title: Singapore High Court sanctioned WazirX restructuring scheme
events.description: Zettai announced that Singapore's High Court sanctioned a creditor-approved restructuring scheme, paving the way for WazirX to restart operations within ten business days of effectiveness.
events.notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no restructuring-approved enum.

events.title: WazirX restarted trading after long suspension
events.description: WazirX restarted trading on October 24, 2025, roughly 15-16 months after the July 2024 hack, with phased market activation and zero-fee restart offers.
events.notes: Restart marker supports active classification; some reports noted withdrawals remained constrained during early restart.

events.title: Phemex detected a hot-wallet security incident
events.description: Phemex detected unusual activity in its hot wallet and described the incident as a hacker attack, isolating affected devices and contacting security firms and law enforcement.
events.notes: Official source is primary; external estimates of loss are not required for entity classification.

events.title: Phemex temporarily suspended deposits and withdrawals
events.description: Phemex suspended deposits and withdrawals across chains as an emergency response to the hot-wallet incident, then resumed chains and assets in staged updates.
events.notes: Official timeline gives asset-by-asset restoration dates.

events.title: Phemex withdrawal services were restored
events.description: Phemex's final update stated that all withdrawal services were restored in February 2025 after the hot-wallet incident response and system security upgrades.
events.notes: Month-level restoration marker based on official final update.

events.title: Upbit suffered a Solana hot-wallet incident
events.description: Upbit detected abnormal withdrawals of Solana-network assets, with reporting estimating roughly USD 30-40 million or 54 billion KRW in assets transferred to an unauthorized external wallet.
events.notes: Loss figures vary by source and exchange update; keep approximate range.

events.title: Upbit suspended deposits and withdrawals after abnormal activity
events.description: Upbit urgently suspended digital-asset deposits and withdrawals after detecting abnormal activity involving Solana-network tokens and moved remaining assets to cold wallets.
events.notes: Temporary suspension; not a death event.

events.title: Upbit scheduled Solana-related deposit and withdrawal resumption
events.description: Upbit announced that deposits and withdrawals for Solana network-related digital assets and SOL staking services would resume sequentially from December 4, 2025.

evidence.title: SEC Charges Entities Operating Crypto Asset Trading Platform Mango Markets
evidence.notes: Primary regulatory source for settled charges, penalties, token-destruction / delisting obligations, and unregistered-broker allegations.

evidence.title: Mango Markets shuts down following SEC settlement
evidence.notes: Used for shutdown announcement, Solana DEX classification, and governance-proposal wind-down context.

evidence.title: Mango Markets To Wind Down Operations After $110 Million Exploit and SEC Settlement
evidence.notes: Supporting source for Jan. 13 proposal effectiveness, 2022 exploit background, and platform wind-down framing.

evidence.title: Iran crypto exchange hit by hackers, $90 million destroyed
evidence.notes: Primary source for Nobitex hack, approximate $90M destroyed, political-motivation context, and offline website/app state.

evidence.title: Hackers steal and destroy millions from Iran's largest crypto exchange
evidence.notes: Supporting source for hot-wallet breach, website/app unavailability, and Elliptic burn analysis.

evidence.title: Pro-Israel hackers destroy $90 million in Iran crypto exchange breach, analytics firm says
evidence.notes: Supporting source for Elliptic attribution, fund-burning mechanism, and exchange-scale context.

evidence.title: Singapore Court Sanctions Zettai's Creditor-Approved Restructuring Scheme
evidence.notes: Primary source for court-sanctioned restructuring and restart pathway.

evidence.title: WazirX to resume trading on Friday, ending 16-month suspension from hack
evidence.notes: Used for restart date, 16-month suspension, and $230M hack context.

evidence.title: WazirX restarts exchange operations tomorrow, 15 months after a crippling cyberattack
evidence.notes: Supporting source for 15-month shutdown, restart timeline, and phased activation.

evidence.title: WazirX Resumes Trading After 16-Month Shutdown, But Withdrawals Still Locked
evidence.notes: Supporting source for long withdrawal/trading interruption and early restart limitations.

evidence.title: Phemex Hot Wallet Security Incident Update and Timeline
evidence.notes: Primary official source for Phemex hack detection, emergency suspension, staged restoration, and final withdrawal-service restoration.

evidence.title: Phemex hot wallet exploit drains millions from crypto exchange
evidence.notes: Secondary source for exploit / hot-wallet incident framing.

evidence.title: Korea's Upbit Suspends Deposit And Withdrawal Service After Abnormal Activity in Solana Tokens
evidence.notes: Used for Upbit Solana abnormal-withdrawal incident, transfer suspension, loss estimate, and reimbursement statement.

evidence.title: Upbit suffers $37 million hack on Solana assets, halts withdrawals
evidence.notes: Supporting source for KRW/USD loss estimate, affected Solana assets, and withdrawal/deposit halt.

evidence.title: [Upbit] Notice of Scheduled Resumption of Deposits and Withdrawals for Solana Network-related Digital Assets and SOL Staking
evidence.notes: Used for scheduled sequential resumption of Solana-network deposits/withdrawals and SOL staking.

manual_review_checklist: Confirm no existing Mango Markets, Nobitex, WazirX, Phemex, or Upbit records before canonical apply; if existing, preserve entity and append/update events.
Apply Mango Markets as dead / regulation with 2025-01-13 as terminal wind-down marker.
Apply Nobitex as limited, not dead, unless a permanent closure marker is later found.
Apply WazirX as active despite long 2024-2025 suspension because October 2025 restart is documented.
Apply Phemex and Upbit as active with temporary service-suspension and restoration events.
Use event ids hei_ev_000382 through hei_ev_000396 only if available.
Use evidence ids hei_src_000790 through hei_src_000804 only if available.
